context: Tests whether the response provides a sound Redis caching design for a read-heavy, infrequently-updated catalog.

checklist.name: Proposes cache key structure
checklist.description: Defines a sensible cache key pattern that accounts for pagination, filters, or query parameters

checklist.name: Sets appropriate TTL
checklist.description: Suggests a TTL that balances freshness vs performance given the few-times-per-day update frequency

checklist.name: Describes invalidation strategy
checklist.description: Explains how to invalidate cache when products are updated (event-driven, key deletion, versioned keys, or pattern-based)

checklist.name: Addresses cache stampede or thundering herd
checklist.description: Mentions the risk of many concurrent requests hitting the DB when cache expires and suggests mitigation (locking, stale-while-revalidate, jittered TTL)

checklist.name: No incorrect information
checklist.description: Response does not contain factually wrong statements about Redis or caching patterns
